-- 3 of 7 --

SPEEDWRITING TIPS
‣ STEP 1: 	10 Minutes Of Silence
No phones, no internet, nothing but running water (as in a cold
shower) And no, you don’t always have to do this (but do it NOW)
‣ 	STEP 2: 	Feel Into The “3 Modes”
There’s the “real you”. There’s the 	character writer 	(the one free
from drama, troubles, or neurosis). Then there’s the 	avatar 	(you
must write TO ONE AVATAR, and a picture helps.)
‣ STEP 3: 	Tap Into Character Mode
You are not writing as “you”. You are writing as 	your ideal
character and self. 	Free from restraints.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

SPEEDWRITING TIPS
‣ STEP 4: 	50 Minute Timer Set
You will plan to write for 50 minutes without interruptions. You will
not edit. You will not think about formulas, structure, or research.
You will WRITE.
‣ 	STEP 5: 	Limit Yourself To 4 “Sets”
You will write for 50 minutes, rest for 10. Eat, pee, kiss your dog,
but do NOT BREAK CHARACTER.
‣ STEP 6: 	Read Your Work To Your Avatar’s Picture
After 50 minutes, read what you wrote “to” your avatar. If every
word sounds like it’s written 	to that one person, you’re on the
right track.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

SPEEDWRITING TIPS
‣ STEP 7: 	Step Away For A Day
Don’t even look at what you wrote for 24 hours.
‣ 	STEP 8: 	Record Your Writing
Record what you wrote, again speaking 	to the avatar. 	Anything
that sounds like something you would not say to him/her, cut it.
‣ STEP 9: 	Edit
Now the hard work begins: edit your work by trimming words. Cut
every word out you can while keeping the meaning of the sentence.
‣ STEP 10: 	Publish
Fearlessly 	present your work to your client.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
